title: Fetch - static open-web fetcher (httpx + UA + timeout + retry)
description:
Session 3 (Fetch) chain, first link. Distilled from `REPO_CONTENT.md` + `docs/s3-fetch-design.md`. The `FetchResult` contract (GH-11, `scholarship_factory/fetch.py`) is merged; this ticket adds the function that produces one: `fetch_url(url) -> FetchResult` over static HTTP. No adapters, no politeness/robots, no caching, no JS rendering - those are separate tickets.

## Locked decisions (honor; do not re-litigate)

- **Static `httpx` GET only.** Headless/Playwright is explicitly deferred until a real fixture proves a static fetch returns an empty SPA shell.
- **Browser-like `User-Agent` by default.** lablab.ai's 403 is already diagnosed as a UA check, not real auth. A UA override is the fix; **no fingerprint-spoofing arms race** beyond that.
- **Retry = exponential backoff (`tenacity`) on 429 / 5xx / timeouts / transport errors, bounded attempts (e.g. 3).** 4xx (other than 429) is a hard fail - never retry a 404/403.
- **Every outcome is represented honestly in `FetchResult`** - never raise through to the caller for an expected web failure: non-2xx -> `status_code` set, `ok` False; timeout/transport error after retries -> `error` populated, `status_code` None. Follow redirects; record `final_url`.
- Sensible default timeout (~15s), overridable.

## Scope

1. `fetch_url(url, *, timeout=..., user_agent=...) -> FetchResult` in `scholarship_factory/` (extend `fetch.py` or a sibling module - match repo conventions).
2. Add `tenacity` as a dependency via uv (`pyproject.toml`).
3. Unit tests using `httpx.MockTransport` (no real network).
acceptance_criteria: 404 and 403 -> exactly one attempt (no retry), `ok` False, `status_code` recorded, no exception.
429 then 200 (mock sequence) -> retried, final result `ok` True.
Persistent timeout/transport error -> bounded retries then an honest `FetchResult` with `error` set and `status_code` None; no exception escapes.
The request carries the browser-like `User-Agent` header (asserted in the mock).
A redirect records the followed `final_url` while preserving `requested_url`.
All tests offline (`httpx.MockTransport`); `uv run pytest -q` green.
